operating_state: Total revenues declined to $50.8 billion from $54.4 billion driven by lower commodity prices despite higher volumes. Upstream earnings decreased to $5.2 billion from $6.9 billion due to lower crude oil and natural gas realizations globally. Downstream earnings surged to $1.8 billion from $331 million driven by significantly higher refining margins, with international downstream swinging from loss to profit. Net oil-equivalent production decreased 3 percent to 2.98 million barrels per day primarily due to the end of the Erawan concession in Thailand.
cost_structure: Purchased crude oil and products costs decreased to $29.4 billion from $33.4 billion reflecting lower commodity price environment. Operating expenses increased to $6.0 billion from $5.6 billion driven by higher transportation expenses and services fees, partially offset by lower employee benefit expenses. Depreciation, depletion and amortization decreased to $3.5 billion from $3.7 billion due to lower production and rates.
material_events: Board authorized new $75 billion share repurchase program effective April 2023, replacing the previous $25 billion program. Company returned $6.5 billion to shareholders through dividends at $1.51 per share and share repurchases. Capital expenditures increased to $3.0 billion from $2.0 billion with higher upstream spending. Achieved first oil at Mad Dog 2 project in Gulf of Mexico.
macro_exposures: California Senate Bill SBx1-2 signed March 2023 authorizes establishment of maximum gross gasoline refining margins and associated fees with uncertain timing and impact. Continued exposure to sanctions affecting Caspian Pipeline Consortium operations potentially impacting Tengiz field in Kazakhstan. Venezuela operations resumed crude oil liftings under U.S. License 41 with positive contribution to results.
forward_outlook: Company expects to repurchase $4.375 billion of common stock during second quarter 2023 under new authorization while maintaining focus on delivering higher returns, lower carbon and superior shareholder value. Management continues developing oil and gas resources while growing lower carbon businesses in renewable fuels, hydrogen, carbon capture and other emerging technologies.
uncertainty_profile: Effective tax rate subject to substantial volatility during earnings fluctuations due to jurisdictional mix effects with tax examinations remaining open across major jurisdictions. Credit loss allowance of $951 million concentrated in non-trade receivables including government joint venture partners. Environmental contingencies exist across operating, closed and divested sites with future costs not fully determinable.
